vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1554

plan.title: installer: add maintainer mode to avoid consumer file projections in framework repo

narratives.Description: Add a maintainer-specific installer path for directive framework checkouts so maintainers can bootstrap tools without projecting consumer files into the repository. The story covers Linux/WSL maintainer setup, consumer behavior preservation, and documentation that clearly separates maintainer and consumer installer flows.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: Update the Go installer maintainer-mode flow to bootstrap or fail loud for maintainer-required tools while skipping consumer file projections in framework checkouts. Extend setup tests and installer/docs coverage for maintainer-mode JSON, consumer-mode compatibility, and the optional `ghx` caching-tool distinction.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive maintainer, I want the installer to bootstrap maintainer tools without projecting consumer files, so that framework checkouts stay clean while Linux setup remains fast.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary
When the published Linux installer is run against the directive framework repository itself, it treats the checkout like a consumer project and rewrites/deposits consumer-managed files. Maintainer checkouts need a first-class installer mode that bootstraps tools and payload checks without replacing maintainer-owned project files.

Observed during Ubuntu/WSL validation of `deft-install v0.43.0`:

```bash
/home/msadams/install-linux-amd64 --yes --upgrade --repo-root /home/msadams/repos/deft/directive --json
```

The Linux bootstrap fix worked: missing `gh`, `task`, and `uv` were installed successfully. But the installer also staged consumer-style changes in the framework repo, including:

- `AGENTS.md` managed-section header rewritten toward consumer-installed shape
- `.gitignore` additions for `.deft/core.bak-*`, `.deft/*.bak-*`, and `vbrief/*.lock`
- `.gitattributes` additions marking `.deft/core/**` as generated/vendored
- consumer support files such as `greptile.json`, CodeQL config, core guard workflow, and lifecycle `.gitkeep` files

For a maintainer checkout, `AGENTS.md`, `.gitignore`, and `.gitattributes` must remain the repository-maintainer versions from the checkout, not the consumer installer projections.

## Proposal
Add an explicit maintainer mode to `cmd/deft-install`, for example:

```bash
deft-install --yes --upgrade --maintainer --repo-root /path/to/directive --json
```

## Expected behavior
In `--maintainer` mode, the installer should:

- Still perform Linux core-tool bootstrap in `--yes` mode (`gh`, `task`, `uv`) and report missing unsupported tools clearly.
- Detect that `--repo-root` is the directive framework repository or accept the explicit `--maintainer` flag as authority.
- Preserve maintainer-owned files such as `AGENTS.md`, `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes`, `Taskfile.yml`, repo workflows, and repo-local policy files unless a maintainer-specific update path explicitly owns them.
- Avoid depositing consumer-only guard/config files into the maintainer repo unless they are already part of the repo source of truth.
- Emit JSON that records `maintainer_mode: true` and lists any skipped consumer projections.

## Acceptance criteria
- Given a directive framework checkout and `--maintainer`, when the Linux installer runs with `--yes --upgrade --json`, then it bootstraps missing core tools but leaves `AGENTS.md`, `.gitignore`, and `.gitattributes` byte-for-byte unchanged.
- Given a consumer project and no `--maintainer`, existing consumer install behavior remains unchanged.
- Given `--maintainer` outside a directive framework checkout, the installer fails loud or emits an actionable warning rather than silently applying the wrong projection.
- Tests cover maintainer mode in `cmd/deft-install/setup_test.go` with fixture assertions for skipped consumer projections and unchanged maintainer files.

## Labels
Suggested: `installer`, `enhancement`, `source-of-truth`.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a directive framework checkout, when `deft-install --yes --upgrade --maintainer --repo-root <checkout> --json` runs, then `git status --short` shows no installer-created changes to tracked maintainer-owned files.
Given a consumer project, when the installer runs without `--maintainer`, then the existing consumer projection files are still created or updated and the consumer install tests continue to pass.
Given maintainer mode, when JSON output is requested, then the response includes separate maintainer-tool status for `go`, `node`, and `ghx` without marking those tools required for consumer mode.

items.title: Document maintainer and consumer setup flows

narrative.Acceptance: Given the install docs, when a maintainer reads setup guidance, then README links to CONTRIBUTING for framework setup and getting-started does not duplicate maintainer instructions.

swarm.missing_traces_justification: Issue #1554 and its follow-up comments define the maintainer-mode installer scope and acceptance refinements.

references.title: Issue #1554: installer: add maintainer mode to avoid consumer file projections in framework repo
